download_details.title: Vera Rubin – Extreme Co-Design: An Evolution from Grace Blackwell Oberon
Inside the NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform: Six New Chips, One AI Supercomputer | NVIDIA Technical Blog
Infrastructure for Scalable AI Reasoning | NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform
Rack-Scale Agentic AI Supercomputer | NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72

findings: The Rubin GPU integrates 224 Streaming Multiprocessors with fifth-generation Tensor Cores optimized for NVFP4 and FP8, scaling to 336B transistors compared to Blackwell's 208B-transistor chip, and achieves up to 50 PFLOPS NVFP4 inference vs. 10 PFLOPS for Blackwell.

The Rubin GPU adopts HBM4 memory with doubled interface width versus HBM3e, providing up to 288 GB capacity per GPU and aggregate bandwidth of up to 22 TB/s, nearly triple Blackwell's memory bandwidth.

The third-generation Transformer Engine introduces hardware-accelerated adaptive compression for NVFP4 performance, enabling up to 50 PetaFLOPS of NVFP4 inference, and is fully backward-compatible with Blackwell-optimized code.
NVLink 6 delivers 3.6 TB/s of bidirectional GPU-to-GPU bandwidth per GPU, doubling the 1,800 GB/s scale-up bandwidth of Blackwell's NVLink 5.

The Vera CPU features 88 custom Olympus cores with Spatial Multithreading (176 threads), up to 1.2 TB/s memory bandwidth, up to 1.5 TB LPDDR5X capacity, and 1.8 TB/s NVLink-C2C, replacing Grace's 72 Neoverse V2 cores, 512 GB/s memory bandwidth, and 900 GB/s NVLink-C2C.
